words.en: the subjects comprising a course of study
words.example: The university revised its curriculum to include more practical skills.

words.en: the method and practice of teaching
words.example: Modern pedagogy emphasises student participation over lecturing.

words.en: relating to skills needed for a particular job
words.example: Vocational training offers a faster path to employment than university.

words.en: having a high reputation; admired
words.example: She was offered a place at a prestigious medical school in London.

words.en: the ability to read and write
words.example: Adult literacy rates have improved significantly over the past decade.

words.en: financial award granted for academic achievement
words.example: He won a full scholarship to study engineering abroad.

words.en: using someone else's work without proper credit
words.example: Universities use specialised software to detect plagiarism in essays.

words.en: the right or ability to govern oneself
words.example: Universities should grant students greater autonomy in choosing modules.

words.en: considering the whole rather than separate parts
words.example: Finland's education system takes a holistic approach to student development.

words.en: a person who leaves education before completing it
words.example: High dropout rates in rural areas remain a major concern.

words.en: a branch of knowledge; also self-control
words.example: Linguistics is a relatively young academic discipline.

words.en: instruction or fees paid for instruction
words.example: Rising tuition fees have made higher education less accessible.

words.en: relating to education and scholarship
words.example: Her academic achievements include three published research papers.

words.en: relating to thought and reasoning
words.example: Reading classical literature provides significant intellectual stimulation.

words.en: the ability to retain information or keep students
words.example: Active learning improves long-term retention of new concepts.

words.en: variety of plant and animal life
words.example: Rainforests contain the highest biodiversity of any habitat on Earth.

words.en: a community of organisms and their environment
words.example: Coral reefs form one of the most fragile ecosystems on the planet.

words.en: ability to maintain ecological balance long-term
words.example: Sustainability has become central to government environmental policy.

words.en: clearing of trees from a large area
words.example: Deforestation in the Amazon has accelerated dramatically since 2019.

words.en: substances released into the air, often pollutants
words.example: Many countries have pledged to halve carbon emissions by 2030.

words.en: naturally replenishable; e.g. solar or wind
words.example: Investment in renewable energy reached a record high last year.

words.en: to reduce the severity of something
words.example: Planting trees can help mitigate the effects of climate change.

words.en: the state of a species no longer existing
words.example: Several frog species are on the brink of extinction.

words.en: to maintain in original state
words.example: National parks were established to preserve natural habitats.

words.en: reduction of resources to inadequate amounts
words.example: The depletion of groundwater threatens agriculture in many regions.

words.en: the natural environment of an organism
words.example: Urban expansion has destroyed the habitat of many local species.

words.en: the action of making something impure or polluted
words.example: Industrial waste has led to severe water contamination downstream.

words.en: natural fuel like coal, oil, or gas
words.example: Transitioning away from fossil fuels is critical to meeting climate goals.

words.en: amount of CO2 produced by activities
words.example: Cycling instead of driving significantly reduces your carbon footprint.

words.en: not harmful to the environment
words.example: More consumers are choosing eco-friendly packaging when shopping.

words.en: introduction of new methods or ideas
words.example: Technological innovation has transformed every major industry.

words.en: use of machines to perform tasks automatically
words.example: Automation has replaced many routine jobs in manufacturing.

words.en: computer systems that simulate human intelligence
words.example: Artificial intelligence is being used to detect diseases earlier than human doctors.

words.en: protection of computer systems from attack
words.example: Companies are investing heavily in cybersecurity after recent data breaches.

words.en: significant development or achievement
words.example: Scientists announced a major breakthrough in renewable battery technology.

words.en: no longer used or useful
words.example: Many smartphones become obsolete within just three years.

words.en: gap between those with and without digital access
words.example: The digital divide widened during the pandemic when learning moved online.

words.en: basic facilities or systems supporting operations
words.example: Reliable internet infrastructure is essential for remote work.

words.en: close observation, often by cameras or data tracking
words.example: Concerns about mass surveillance have grown alongside CCTV expansion.

words.en: widespread; commonly occurring
words.example: Smartphone addiction is increasingly prevalent among teenagers.

words.en: at the forefront of progress
words.example: The hospital invested in cutting-edge imaging equipment.

words.en: the act of combining into a unified whole
words.example: The seamless integration of AI tools into office software has improved productivity.

words.en: process of converting data into a code
words.example: End-to-end encryption protects messages from being read by third parties.

words.en: to use or move into a position
words.example: The company plans to deploy autonomous delivery robots in major cities.

words.en: set of rules to solve a problem
words.example: Social media algorithms decide what content each user sees.

words.en: state of being comfortable, healthy, and happy
words.example: Regular exercise contributes significantly to mental well-being.

words.en: involving little physical activity
words.example: Office workers often lead sedentary lifestyles that harm long-term health.

words.en: persisting for a long time
words.example: Diabetes is a chronic condition that requires lifelong management.

words.en: widespread occurrence of an infectious disease
words.example: Public health officials worked to contain the cholera epidemic.

words.en: the body's ability to resist infection
words.example: A balanced diet helps strengthen the body's immunity.

words.en: the process of providing food for health
words.example: Schools should teach children the basics of good nutrition.

words.en: condition of being grossly overweight
words.example: Childhood obesity rates have tripled over the past three decades.

words.en: long life
words.example: Japan is famous for its population's remarkable longevity.

words.en: treatment with a vaccine to provide immunity
words.example: Childhood vaccination programmes have nearly eliminated polio.

words.en: to make less severe or painful
words.example: Yoga can alleviate stress and improve sleep quality.

words.en: designed to keep something from happening
words.example: Preventive healthcare is cheaper than treating diseases later.

words.en: identification of an illness
words.example: Early diagnosis dramatically improves survival rates for most cancers.

words.en: restoring health after illness or injury
words.example: Physiotherapy plays a central role in post-stroke rehabilitation.

words.en: able to be spread by contact
words.example: Measles is one of the most contagious diseases known.

words.en: lack of proper nutrition
words.example: Malnutrition remains a serious problem in conflict-affected regions.

words.en: the people engaged in or available for work
words.example: Women now make up nearly half of the global workforce.

words.en: rate of output per unit of input
words.example: Remote work has improved productivity for many knowledge workers.

words.en: payment for work or services
words.example: The role offers competitive remuneration plus generous benefits.

words.en: to work from home using technology
words.example: Since the pandemic, more employees telecommute at least three days a week.

words.en: physical or mental collapse from overwork
words.example: Many healthcare workers experienced burnout during the pandemic.

words.en: ranking system in an organisation
words.example: Flatter hierarchies are common in modern tech companies.

words.en: guidance given by an experienced person
words.example: Mentorship programmes help young employees develop their careers.

words.en: temporary work placement to gain experience
words.example: A summer internship at the firm led directly to a full-time offer.

words.en: advancement in rank or position
words.example: She earned a promotion to senior manager within two years.

words.en: tangible output to be produced
words.example: The project deliverables include a report and a working prototype.

words.en: person with interest in a project or organisation
words.example: All stakeholders should be consulted before major decisions.

words.en: loss of job because position is no longer needed
words.example: The company announced two hundred redundancies after the merger.

words.en: feeling of satisfaction from achieving something
words.example: Teaching offers a deep sense of fulfilment despite modest pay.

words.en: equilibrium between work and personal life
words.example: Younger employees increasingly prioritise work-life balance over salary.

words.en: building professional relationships
words.example: Networking events are essential for early-career professionals.
